ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product entry; no specific boneless breaded fried wing combo entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers coated fried chicken meat; no specific boneless wing combo with sides code exists. Combo meal components (fries, drink, dip) are not individually coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for boneless fried chicken wing combo meal. Not clinically coded as a distinct entity.

serving.description: 1 set meal (6 boneless wings, regular fries, ranch dip, 1 drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal comprising six pieces of breaded deep-fried boneless chicken wings, a regular serving of potato fries, a ranch dipping sauce, and one carbonated soft drink, weighing approximately 650 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, wing, frozen, with sauce, NFS (breaded, fried) and French fries, fast food composite
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for a 6-piece boneless wings combo meal exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database. Per-100g values were derived by weighted composite estimation: breaded fried chicken (USDA FDC #748967, ~45% weight), fast-food french fries (USDA FDC #170720, ~30% weight), ranch dressing (USDA FDC #172920, ~5% weight), and carbonated cola-type beverage (USDA FDC #174851, ~20% weight). Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this composite and found broadly consistent; composite-derived values are used as the authoritative per-100g basis. Sodium value is elevated due to seasoned breading and ranch dip contribution.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for breaded fried chicken and french fries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific combo meal. GI estimated as weighted composite: breaded fried chicken GI ~60 (University of Sydney GI Database, analogous entry for battered/breaded fried chicken, ~45% carbohydrate weight contribution), french fries GI ~75 (University of Sydney GI Database, french fries entry, ~50% carbohydrate weight contribution), carbonated cola drink GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, ~5% carbohydrate weight contribution). Weighted composite GI ≈ 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 65 × (10.8 g/100g × 650 g) / 100 = 65 × 70.2 / 100 ≈ 45.6 g GL. Rounded to 45.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (338 mg/100g; ~2197 mg per 650g serving) from seasoned breading, ranch dip, and fries, combined with a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this combo meal due to sodium content approaching or exceeding the daily 2000 mg limit in a single serving; if consumed, replace the carbonated drink with plain water, request reduced seasoning, and use ranch dip sparingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~45.5 g GL) driven by refined-carbohydrate breading, potato fries, and sugar-sweetened carbonated drink, with low dietary fibre (0.8 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this combo meal as the combination of high-GI refined carbohydrates, sugar-sweetened beverage, and low fibre content will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting the drink with water and fries with a salad would substantially reduce glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (2.3 g/100g; ~15 g per serving), trans fat (0.08 g/100g), high sodium (~2197 mg per serving), and cholesterol (38 mg/100g) from deep-frying and ranch dressing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this meal due to its combined saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium burden, all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk; if consumed occasionally, portion size should be strictly limited and the drink replaced with water.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; sugar-sweetened drink and trans fat further increase pro-inflammatory load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried breaded chicken and sugar-sweetened beverages as AGEs and dietary trans fats are established pro-inflammatory agents that may exacerbate synovial inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 6 Piece Boneless Wings Combo is a Western-style fast food set meal sold in Singapore, consisting of six pieces of boneless chicken leg meat that are lightly coated in seasoned wheat flour breading and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The combo is served with a regular portion of salted potato fries, a creamy ranch dipping sauce, and a carbonated soft drink. The chicken pieces are tender and juicy on the inside with a crunchy exterior, carrying a mildly savory and lightly spiced flavor profile. The meal is classified as halal and is commonly associated with fast food dining occasions. Nutritionally, the combo is energy-dense at approximately 185 kcal per 100g and 1202 kcal per 650g serving, with moderate protein (10 g/100g), high fat (11.5 g/100g including 2.3 g saturated fat and trace trans fat), and moderate refined carbohydrates (10.8 g/100g). Sodium is notably high at 338 mg/100g, equating to approximately 2197 mg per full serving, which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit for adults. Dietary fibre is low at 0.8 g/100g. The glycemic load per serving is high (~45.5 g), driven by the breaded coating, potato fries, and sugar-sweetened drink. This meal is not recommended for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, refined carbohydrate, and pro-inflammatory AGE content from deep-frying.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific combo meal exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database. Per-100g nutrient values were derived by weighted composite estimation from four USDA FDC component entries (breaded fried chicken, french fries, ranch dressing, carbonated cola beverage). The input record values (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were broadly consistent with the composite estimate and have been superseded by the USDA-derived composite. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component entries. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries: breaded fried chicken (FDC #748967, ~45% weight), fast-food french fries (FDC #170720, ~30% weight), ranch dressing (FDC #172920, ~5% weight), and carbonated cola beverage (FDC #174851, ~20% weight). No exact approved-source entry for this combo meal exists. 2. The input record listed two portions both with 650g and both marked is_default=true; the 6-piece variant (650g) is used as the canonical serving size. The 8-piece variant label appears to be a data entry error in the source record as the gram weight is identical. 3. Vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite fast food meal is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database at the combo-meal level as of 2026-04-16. 4. Monounsaturated fat (4.8 g/100g) and polyunsaturated fat (3.1 g/100g) are estimated from USDA FDC component fatty acid profiles for breaded fried chicken and french fries. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.08 g/100g consistent with input record; this is a trace level from frying oil but is clinically relevant for rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease. 6. The combo includes a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage (SSB), which triggers hard disqualification gates for diabetes, hypertension, and rheumatoid arthritis health suitability assessments. 7. GI of 65 (medium) is a weighted analogous estimate; actual GI may vary depending on specific sauce flavors chosen and drink type. 8. Halal status is carried from input record cultural.dietary_tags; independent halal certification verification was not performed in this workflow.
